name: FAQs | openEuler
description: Get answers to frequently asked questions about openEuler.

acceptedAnswer.text: The OpenAtom openEuler project, short for openEuler, is an open-source OS project incubated and operated by the OpenAtom Foundation. It is a digital infrastructure OS that fits into any server, cloud computing, edge computing, and embedded deployment. This Linux distribution is compatible with multiple instruction set architectures and ideal for a wide range of operational technology applications, enabling OT-ICT convergence.

mainEntity.name: What is the openEuler community like?

acceptedAnswer.text: Established officially on December 31, 2019, the openEuler community operates as a global hub for developers worldwide, aiming to foster an open, diverse, and architecture-inclusive software ecosystem tailored for wide-ranging digital infrastructure needs. openEuler collaborates closely with both upstream and downstream communities to ensure continuous tech improvement and timely release of new versions.

mainEntity.name: What instruction set architectures does openEuler support?

acceptedAnswer.text: With active collaboration from leading chip vendors like Intel and AMD, openEuler supports multiple processor architectures, including x86, Arm, SW64, RISC-V, and LoongArch, with plans to expand to PowerPC. openEuler is optimized for a wide range of CPU chips, such as Loongson 3 series, Zhaoxin KaiXian and KaiSheng, Intel Sierra Forest and Granite Rapids, and AMD EPYC Milan and Genoa. openEuler's compatibility extends beyond the CPU, encompassing NIC, RAID, Fibre Channel, GPU & AI, DPU, SSD, and security cards. By offering a unified OS that can run on various devices, openEuler facilitates streamlined application development, allowing developers to target a wide range of hardware without significant code modification.

mainEntity.name: How often does openEuler release a new version?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler releases two types of community versions: long-term support (LTS) and innovation versions. LTS versions provide long-term stability and support, while innovation versions deliver the latest features and technologies.
